# CoolMyMac

> An advanced, native macOS utility with a beautiful Menu Bar UI and CLI to monitor temperatures and control your Mac's fans.

## About
CoolMyMac is a modern, Swift-based macOS application built to give users control over their thermal management on both Apple Silicon and Intel Macs. It replaces legacy, bloated tools with a native SwiftUI interface and a powerful CLI.

## Key Features
- **Apple Silicon & Intel Support**: Dynamically interfaces with IOKit (`AppleSMC`) to read sensor data and override fan modes natively on both architectures.
- **Dynamic Menu Bar**: The icon shifts from blue to red as CPU/GPU temperatures increase.
- **Profiles**: Built-in cooling profiles (Quiet, Balanced, Performance, Max) that automatically ramp up fan RPM based on weighted thermal sensors.
- **CLI (`coolmymac`)**: A dedicated command-line interface to read thermals (`coolmymac temps`) and control profiles programmatically.

## Directory Structure
```
CoolMyMac/
├── CoolMyMac-App/        # The main Xcode project
│   ├── App/              # The SwiftUI MenuBar application
│   └── Daemon/           # The root LaunchDaemon (handles IOKit writes)
├── CoolMyMac-CLI/        # The Swift Package for the command-line tool
├── SMCKit/               # Core framework for interfacing with the macOS SMC
└── scripts/              # Build scripts and utilities
```

## Architecture Constraints
- **Universal Binary**: The CLI (`CoolMyMac-CLI`) is embedded as a Universal Binary in the main App bundle during the build phase. Do not configure builds that are strictly `arm64` as Intel support is required.
- **App/Daemon Split**: The Daemon runs as `root` via `SMAppService`. The App and CLI run unprivileged and communicate with the Daemon securely via XPC (`CoolMyMacXPCProtocol.swift`).
- **SMCKit**: A robust, abstracted Swift package that safely queries and commands the macOS SMC (System Management Controller). It must remain agnostic to the process it is running in.

## Installation
The recommended installation method for users is Homebrew:
`brew tap ecc521/coolmymac`
`brew install --cask coolmymac`

Alternatively, signed DMG files for drag-and-drop installation are available on the [GitHub Releases](https://github.com/ecc521/CoolMyMac/releases) page.
